description:
You are a second-line support engineer who was asked to collect very specific
    diagnostics about a legacy text file that is still stored in the
    single-byte ISO-8859-1 encoding.  
    1. Convert the file  
            /home/user/support/input/legacy_notes.txt  
       from ISO-8859-1 to UTF-8 and save the converted copy to  
            /home/user/support/converted/legacy_notes_utf8.txt  
       Do NOT add a UTF-8 BOM. The textual content after the conversion must
       still read exactly (visually)  

            Café Münster - réunion à 14h.

       and be terminated by a single Unix newline (LF, 0x0A).  
    2. Calculate the SHA-256 checksums of BOTH the original file (still
       ISO-8859-1) and the newly created UTF-8 file.  
    3. Write a plain-text ASCII log called  
            /home/user/support/encoding_diagnostics.log  
       whose content must be **exactly** (line–for–line, no extra spaces,
       no trailing blanks) the following template, with the two placeholder
       values replaced by the real hexadecimal checksums you just computed:  

            === Encoding Conversion Report ===
            SourceFile:/home/user/support/input/legacy_notes.txt
            SourceEncoding:ISO-8859-1
            DestinationFile:/home/user/support/converted/legacy_notes_utf8.txt
            DestinationEncoding:UTF-8
            SourceSHA256:<ACTUAL_SOURCE_HASH>
            DestinationSHA256:<ACTUAL_DESTINATION_HASH>
            === End ===

       Notes for the required formatting:  
         • Every line ends with a single LF (no CR).  
         • There must be **no** blank line before, after, or in-between the
           lines shown above.  
         • Do not insert any spaces after the colons other than what is shown.  
         • The two checksum placeholders have to be replaced by the full
           64-character lowercase hexadecimal digests produced by the
           `sha256sum` utility (i.e. the digest only, without the filename).  

    The job is finished when:
      a) /home/user/support/converted/legacy_notes_utf8.txt exists, is valid
         UTF-8 (no BOM) and displays the same text as the source file, and
      b) /home/user/support/encoding_diagnostics.log exists and follows the
         template above with checksums that match the *actual* files on disk.

    You do not need any root privileges and there are only 2-3 shell commands
    involved (file conversion, checksum calculation, log creation).  Make sure
    that the directory /home/user/support/converted/ exists before writing the
    converted file and that all paths and filenames match those given above
    exactly, including letter case.
truth:
Initial state that exists when the scenario starts:

    1. Directory structure
       /home/user/support/
       └── input/
           └── legacy_notes.txt         (ISO-8859-1 encoded)

    2. Binary contents of /home/user/support/input/legacy_notes.txt
       (byte values shown in hexadecimal, one byte per pair):

           43 61 66 E9 20 4D FC 6E 73 74 65 72 20 2D 20
           72 E9 75 6E 69 6F 6E 20 E0 20 31 34 68 2E 0A

       which, when decoded as ISO-8859-1, displays:

           Café Münster - réunion à 14h.\n

    3. Permissions
       All files and directories are owned by the container user
       (uid/gid == user:user) and are writable (0755 for directories,
       0644 for files).

    Required final state for automated verification:

    A) /home/user/support/converted/legacy_notes_utf8.txt
       • Must exist and be encoded in UTF-8 without BOM.
       • When read as UTF-8, must contain exactly the 30 visible
         characters plus one LF shown in the task:  
             "Café Münster - réunion à 14h.\n"
       • The file’s SHA-256 digest will be computed at test time.

    B) /home/user/support/encoding_diagnostics.log
       • Must exist.
       • Must have the 8-line structure given in the public task with
         BOTH checksum lines containing the correct lowercase 64-character
         hex digests that match the files currently on disk.
       • No additional whitespace or blank lines.

    The grading script will:
       1. Confirm that the converted file is valid UTF-8 and byte-for-byte
          decodes to the required text.
       2. Re-calculate both SHA-256 hashes and verify that the log file
          contains exactly these values.
       3. Ensure the entire log conforms to the strict format above.
